content: Claude explained the MCP (Model Context Protocol) and how it enables cross-LLM memory synchronization through a unified server interface.
Implemented client-side E2E encryption using PBKDF2 key derivation and AES-256-GCM cipher. Server stores only ciphertext.
Fixed MCP server build failure by removing redundant Prisma schema from packages/server. Server now imports PrismaClient from @unimatrix/db.
Xenova/BGE-small local embeddings are working. Replaces Voyage AI, reduces monthly costs from $60 to $0.
Unimatrix SBIR Phase 1 focus: proprietary secure librarian AI with local embeddings, client-side encryption, and fine-tuned model. No external API calls.
Set up Fly.io deployment pipeline with automatic CI/CD triggers on main branch push. FLY_API_TOKEN configured in GitHub secrets.
React hook dependency array issue: useEffect was missing a dependency. Fixed by adding all functions and variables used inside the hook to the dependency array.
Compared HuggingFace Spaces (free GPU) vs modal.com ($0.30/run) for fine-tuning Librarian. HF free tier preferred for Phase 1.
Memory export script prepared for fine-tuning: extracts content, context, tags, importance. Output format: JSONL for training pipeline.
Cross-device continuity achieved: user can start conversation with ChatGPT on iPhone, pick up iPad, and Claude continues with full context via MCP server.
